tests.source_program:
define i64 @lsb_mask_sign_sext_i64(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %sub = add i64 %x, -1
  %not = xor i64 %x, -1
  %and = and i64 %sub, %not
  %shr = ashr i64 %and, 63
  ret i64 %shr
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only shows i32 examples; this tests i64 width which is not explicitly covered in the known test cases

alive2.src:
define i64 @lsb_mask_sign_sext_i64(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %sub = add i64 %x, -1
  %not = xor i64 %x, -1
  %and = and i64 %sub, %not
  %shr = ashr i64 %and, 63
  ret i64 %shr
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i64 @lsb_mask_sign_sext_i64(i64 %x) {
entry:
  %0 = icmp eq i64 %x, 0
  %shr = sext i1 %0 to i64
  ret i64 %shr
}

tests.source_program:
define i32 @lsb_mask_sign_sext_used_in_add(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %sub = add i32 %x, -1
  %not = xor i32 %x, -1
  %and = and i32 %sub, %not
  %shr = ashr i32 %and, 31
  %result = add i32 %shr, %y
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @lsb_mask_sign_sext_used_in_add(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %x, 0
  %ext = sext i1 %cmp to i32
  %result = add i32 %ext, %y
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary shows direct return of shift result; this tests the pattern where the shifted value is used in further arithmetic
